## 1. Overview Below is a **self‑contained Python module** called `report_generator.py` that * Uses **Jinja2** (a popular, battle‑tested templating engine) to render HTML reports. * Provides a **base template** with header/footer that can be inherited by any number of child templates. * Renders **data tables** from a `list[dict]` (each dict is a row; the keys are column names). * Highlights rows that exceed a user‑supplied threshold (via a simple inline Jinja2 conditional or a custom filter). * Allows you to create new report types simply by writing a new child template that extends the base. > **Why Jinja2?** > Jinja2 is the de‑facto standard for templating in the Python ecosystem. > It supports template inheritance, filters, macros, auto‑escaping, and has a very friendly syntax. > (If you prefer Mako, the same logic works – you just switch the environment and loader – but the example below keeps things simple.) --- ## 2. The Module ```python # report_generator.py """ A tiny, opinionated reporting engine that turns a list of dictionaries into an HTML report using Jinja2 templates. Features -------- - Base template (header/footer) with inheritance - Render data tables from list[dict] - Conditional row highlighting via thresholds - Simple API: ReportGenerator.render(template_name, data, context) - Write output to disk Dependencies ------------ pip install Jinja2 """ from __future__ import annotations import os from pathlib import Path from typing import Any, Dict, Iterable, List, Optional import jinja2 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # # 1️⃣ Templates # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # # These are bundled with the module for demo purposes. In production # you might keep them in a "templates" directory and load them from the # filesystem instead. BASE_TEMPLATE = """ {{ title or "Report" }}

{{ header or "" }}

{% block content %}{% endblock %}

{{ footer or "" }}

""" SALES_REPORT_TEMPLATE = """ {% extends "base.html" %} {% block content %}

{{ report_title }}

{% for col in columns %} {% endfor %} {% for row in data %} {% set row_class = "" %} {% if threshold_col and row[threshold_col] > threshold_value %} {% set row_class = "highlight" %} {% endif %} {% for col in columns %} {% endfor %} {% endfor %}
{{ col }}
{{ row[col] }}
{% endblock %} """ # For demo purposes only – you can add as many child templates as you like TEMPLATES = { "base.html": BASE_TEMPLATE, "sales_report.html": SALES_REPORT_TEMPLATE, } # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # # 2️⃣ ReportGenerator class # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # class ReportGenerator: """ Generate HTML reports from a list of dictionaries. Parameters ---------- template_dir : str | None Path to a directory that contains your Jinja2 templates. If None, the bundled templates in :data:`TEMPLATES` are used. """ def __init__(self, template_dir: Optional[str] = None) -> None: if template_dir: loader = jinja2.FileSystemLoader(template_dir) else: loader = jinja2.DictLoader(TEMPLATES) self.env = jinja2.Environment( loader=loader, autoescape=True, ) # Optional: expose a custom filter for threshold-based highlighting self.env.filters["threshold_class"] = self._threshold_class_filter # --------------------------------------------------------------------- # # Public API # --------------------------------------------------------------------- # def render( self, template_name: str, data: List[Dict[str, Any]], *, context: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None, ) -> str: """ Render a template with the supplied data. Parameters ---------- template_name : str Name of the template (e.g. "sales_report.html"). data : list[dict] The table data. Each dict represents a row. context : dict, optional Extra variables you want to pass to the template. The following are automatically added: - data - columns (derived from the first row) - title, header, footer, report_title, threshold_col, threshold_value etc. Returns ------- str The rendered HTML. """ if not data: raise ValueError("Empty data set") # Derive columns from the first row columns = list(data[0].keys()) ctx: Dict[str, Any] = {"data": data, "columns": columns} if context: ctx.update(context) template = self.env.get_template(template_name) return template.render(ctx) def write(self, output_path: str, html: str) -> None: """ Write the rendered HTML to disk. Parameters ---------- output_path : str Path where the file will be written. html : str The HTML content. """ Path(output_path).write_text(html, encoding="utf-8") # --------------------------------------------------------------------- # # Helpers # --------------------------------------------------------------------- # @staticmethod def _threshold_class_filter(value: Any, threshold: Any) -> str: """Return CSS class name if value meets threshold.""" return "highlight" if value > threshold else "" # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # # 3️⃣ Demo / Usage # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # if __name__
